title: Kuwahara Grasshopper 1 is a Throwback 90s MTB
content:
Kuwahara has been on a kick lately. We first reported on the Japanese brand’s Elk Bar, a throwback to the Ritchey Bullmoose, and this week, it announced the Grasshopper 1, a vintage-inspired rim brake MTB. Let’s check it out…

There isn’t much information on Kuwahara’s website, other than it’s a 26″ wheeled vintage-inspired frame with 2.3″ clearance, 100 x 135 spacing, a 1 1/8″ head tube, square taper cranks, and rim brakes. There are two builds, three finishing options (black, blue, and raw) and either silver components for around $800 and a black parts build for around $900.

Build Spec:

  • Fork 4130 Cr-Mo Fork
  • Rims 26″ x 1.75-2.125 Double Wall Alloy Rim 32H
  • Hubs Kuwahara “Twin-Turbine” hubs 32H Front 100mmx9mm / Rear 135mmx10mm
  • Tires Kenda K-1027 26” x 2.1”
  • Brakes DIA-COMPE DC-980 Cantilever Brake Black / Silver
  • Brake Lever DIA-COMPE SS-6 Brake Levers Black / Silver
  • Crankset Alloy Crank BCD : 110mm / 170mm with Chainring 40T
  • Bottom Bracket BSA Square Taper 68mm
  • Free Wheel 18T CP Finish
  • Chain KMC Z410
  • Pedal M537 Alloy Black / Silver 9/16″
  • Stem Kuwahara Stem EX:90mm / Bar Clamp: 25.4mm
  • Headset Integrated 1 1/8″ Campy type Black
  • Grips Kuwahara Lightning Grips Clear Black
  • Handlebar Alloy Flat Bar W:650mm / BS:6 degree / Bar Clamp: 25.4mm
  • Seatpost 27.2 x 350mm Black / Silver
  • Saddle Kuwahara SX Saddle Black

Like the elk bar, these are only available in Japan. But you can roll over to Kuwahara to check out the Grasshopper 1.

The post Kuwahara Grasshopper 1 is a Throwback 90s MTB appeared first on The Radavist.
author: John Watson
